rationale.rule: When an anonymous block contains only many identical low-vertex polylines on an insulation (INSUL) layer, treat it as an insulation hatch cluster (기타), not a plan fragment or wall geometry; layer semantics plus entity homogeneity outrank block size.

wall_line_handles.reason: long horizontal face line of 50-unit-thick band
parallel return of same long band
second long face line 50 units below
closing vertical of long band end

notes: Anonymous plan fragment ~1220 units wide: a 50-thick double-line band with end jambs, two WIPEOUTs and a nested 국기계양대 (flag-pole holder) block - reads as a railing/parapet or opening strip on an exterior wall, not pure symbol art.

rationale.evidence: 20 LINEs on layer 0 form a long narrow band: two full-width horizontal lines at y=-50.5 and y=-100.5 (e.g. handles 52A1, 52A3) spanning x=±588, closed by short verticals, plus 22-unit end lugs out to ±610 - a plan-view strip about 1220x126 units. One INSERT of the named block 'X-평면도(기본형)$0$국기계양대' (flag-pole holder) and two WIPEOUTs sit on it, which is composed drawing content rather than a standalone legend symbol.
rationale.rule: A mixed anonymous block combining raw LINE geometry at building scale with wipeouts and nested named symbols is a grouped fragment of the floor plan (평면 부분도); the long parallel double lines are candidate wall/railing faces, so wall likelihood is moderate rather than zero.
rationale.counterfactual: If the double band were 100-200 units thick on a wall-named layer I would raise wall_likelihood sharply; if the geometry were tiny or centered on the origin at uniform symbol scale with no nested inserts, I would flip to 심볼.
rationale.uncertainty: A 50-unit band is thin for a structural wall, so it may be a balcony railing, parapet coping, or window frame in plan - the projection gives no layer semantics (all layer 0) or linetype/width to distinguish these; the nested flag-pole block's own contents are also not visible.
